corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: exact-address review must show the finite verb host عَقَلُوا plus هُ object suffix before an authored contribution such as 'they understood it' can be certified
what_failed: A populated hover used the entry infinitive/root prose instead of the finite verb plus attached object contribution.
sarf_lesson: Finite verbs need aspect, subject agreement, root/form, and every attached object suffix before any hover is trusted.
nahw_lesson: The clause can decide the object referent, but the هُ suffix cannot disappear into the verb lemma.
learner_explanation: عَقَلُوهُ is not just 'to understand'. The final هُ means the people understood it/him, and the learner needs that visible suffix accounted for.
drill_prompt: Segment عَقَلُوهُ into verb host, plural subject marker, and هُ object; explain why a dictionary infinitive is unsafe.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: component candidates may explain فَـ, the Form IV host, and هُمْ, but they must not become a whole-token certificate without two-vote proof
what_failed: A readable hover exists, but the row is only component evidence. It needs parse-key proof for fā', Form IV perfect 1cp, and 3mp object suffix before rich certification.
sarf_lesson: Component evidence can teach pieces but cannot certify the whole token or propagate across a family by itself.
nahw_lesson: The fā' function and clause relation may affect the learner-facing wording, so nahw must remain in the gate.
learner_explanation: فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ contains فَ, أَهْلَكْنَا, and هُمْ. The English is plausible, but the graph must prove those pieces before using it as a model row.
drill_prompt: Explain why فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ remains gated even when 'so We destroyed them' looks correct.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: ضَعِيفًا is an accusative indefinite nominal/adjectival token; a verb-entry component cannot certify its syntactic role or rich metadata
what_failed: The visible English may be lexically acceptable, but the token is linked through component evidence under a verb family rather than an exact nominal/adjectival entry and iʿrāb decision.
sarf_lesson: A ṣifa/nominal pattern must be classified as nominal before any verb/root entry can be used as evidence.
nahw_lesson: The accusative role, predicate/state reading, or other iʿrāb function must be reviewed before rich certification.
learner_explanation: ضَعِيفًا teaches an adjectival/nominal contribution ('weak'), not a finite verb. The hover needs case and role proof, not only a good English word.
drill_prompt: For ضَعِيفًا, decide why the token is not a verb and name the case/role evidence still needed.

visible_bad_hover: to be bound by an authority such as faith or judgment
corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: definite genitive noun ٱلدِّينِ needs nominal/case/idafa-role review; do not use a verb infinitive as the token hover
what_failed: A noun token received a verbal/root-family definition instead of a token contribution for the definite nominal form.
sarf_lesson: Lexical nouns and maṣdar-like nominals must not inherit a 'to ...' verb gloss merely because the entry family has a verbal explanation.
nahw_lesson: In phrases such as يَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ, construct/genitive role constrains the English contribution.
learner_explanation: ٱلدِّينِ here is a noun in a construction, not the infinitive 'to be bound'. The learner needs the nominal contribution and its grammatical role.
drill_prompt: Compare دِينُكُمْ and ٱلدِّينِ: identify noun shape, definiteness/possession, and why a verb infinitive is blocked.

visible_bad_hover: Islam, full submission to the Will of Allah.
corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: مُسْلِمَيْنِ is a dual/oblique participial or person-describing token; it needs token contribution such as 'two submitters/Muslims' only after exact morphology review
what_failed: The abstract concept entry was used on a derived nominal/person token without number/case/person-shape accounting.
sarf_lesson: Concept entries cannot replace participle/person morphology. Record pattern, number, gender, and case before authoring the hover.
nahw_lesson: The local dua or predication decides wording, but the token still contributes a shaped noun/participle, not the concept alone.
learner_explanation: مُسْلِمَيْنِ has dual/case morphology. It is not simply the concept 'Islam'; the hover must teach the shaped token contribution.
drill_prompt: Classify مُسْلِمَيْنِ and مُسَلَّمَةٌ: which are person/participle/adjectival forms and why is 'Islam' alone unsafe?

visible_bad_hover: to make a covenant, pledge, or commitment that should be maintained
corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: بِعَهْدِ is bā' plus a majrūr nominal host; it needs PP/attachment review before a token hover such as 'by/with the covenant' can be certified
what_failed: A preposition plus noun token inherited a verb-family definition and lost the bā' relation.
sarf_lesson: A nominal host after attached bā' is not a finite verb; block verb-family prose when the surface is a governed noun.
nahw_lesson: The PP attachment decides whether the relation is by/with/in/according to, so the row needs nahw review.
learner_explanation: بِعَهْدِ includes بِـ and a noun. The hover must preserve the preposition relation and the noun, not a verbal definition of covenanting.
drill_prompt: Segment بِعَهْدِ and explain why a verb gloss is unsafe until jar-majrūr attachment is known.
